_meta.name: Q2 Multi-Domain Test LoreDb
_meta.purpose: Test fixture for the lore_coverage_partial heuristic. 15 verified facts across 3 categories (history, science, culture) — large enough that the heuristic can fire (>=3 claims per category) and the >=50% uncovered threshold is meaningful. Used by the Q2 experiment described in HISTORY.md.
_meta.scope: Late-1990s mainland China + general scientific facts + cross-cultural reference material. All facts are publicly verifiable.
_meta.load_via: Pass parsed JSON as loreDb via --lore datasets/lore.q2-multi-domain.json

tiananmen_square_1989.fact: The Tiananmen Square protests took place in Beijing from April to early June 1989; the People's Liberation Army's clearance operation began on the night of June 3-4, 1989.
tiananmen_square_1989.source: Tiananmen Papers (Zhang Liang ed., 2001); contemporaneous BBC reporting.

deng_southern_tour_1992.fact: Deng Xiaoping's Southern Tour (南巡) of January-February 1992 reaffirmed market-oriented economic reforms and was followed by the 14th Party Congress's adoption of the 'socialist market economy' formulation in October 1992.
deng_southern_tour_1992.source: Ezra Vogel, 'Deng Xiaoping and the Transformation of China' (2011), ch. 23.

wto_accession_2001.fact: The People's Republic of China formally acceded to the World Trade Organization on December 11, 2001, after 15 years of negotiation.
wto_accession_2001.source: WTO official records; Joseph Fewsmith, 'China Since Tiananmen' (2008).

sars_outbreak_2003.fact: Severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS) emerged in Guangdong Province in November 2002; the WHO declared a global health alert in March 2003. China's official cumulative case count by July 2003 was 5,327.
sars_outbreak_2003.source: WHO SARS situation reports (2003); Hong Kong CHP retrospective.

expansion_1999.fact: The 1999 Chinese university enrollment expansion (高校扩招) increased annual undergraduate admissions by 47 percent in a single year (from approximately 1.08 million to 1.59 million).
expansion_1999.source: Ministry of Education of the PRC annual statistical bulletins, 1998-2000.

human_genome_2003.fact: The Human Genome Project was declared substantially complete in April 2003, covering approximately 99% of euchromatic regions with 99.99% accuracy.
human_genome_2003.source: International Human Genome Sequencing Consortium, Nature 431:931 (2004).

covid19_pandemic_who_declaration.fact: The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a global pandemic on March 11, 2020.

first_human_iss.fact: The International Space Station has been continuously occupied since November 2, 2000, when Expedition 1 (Shepherd, Krikalev, Gidzenko) docked.
first_human_iss.source: NASA International Space Station mission records.

chernobyl_1986.fact: The Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant accident occurred on April 26, 1986, at Reactor No. 4 during a safety test.
chernobyl_1986.source: IAEA INSAG-7 report (1992); UNSCEAR 2008 Annex D.

harry_potter_first.fact: The first Harry Potter novel, 'Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone' by J. K. Rowling, was published by Bloomsbury on 26 June 1997.
harry_potter_first.source: Bloomsbury publishing record; British Library catalogue.

google_founded_1998.fact: Google was incorporated in California on September 4, 1998, by Larry Page and Sergey Brin while both were PhD students at Stanford University.
google_founded_1998.source: Google Inc. corporate filings; Steven Levy, 'In the Plex' (2011).

iphone_launch_2007.fact: The first-generation iPhone was announced by Apple on January 9, 2007, and went on sale in the United States on June 29, 2007.

china_one_child_policy_end.fact: China's one-child policy, in force since 1980, was officially ended in October 2015 when the Communist Party announced a universal two-child policy effective January 1, 2016.
china_one_child_policy_end.source: Xinhua News Agency, October 29, 2015; State Council white paper.

gfw_great_firewall.fact: The Great Firewall of China (officially part of the Golden Shield Project, 金盾工程) began deployment in 1998 and reached its first operational stage by 2003.
gfw_great_firewall.source: Shanthi Kalathil & Taylor Boas, 'Open Networks, Closed Regimes' (2003).

wikipedia_launch.fact: Wikipedia was launched on January 15, 2001, by Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger as a complement to the expert-edited Nupedia.
wikipedia_launch.source: Wikipedia internal archives; Andrew Lih, 'The Wikipedia Revolution' (2009).
